disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden, rapidly progressive swelling of the lips and face developing over about 2 hours
trigger_question_examples: How quickly did the swelling come on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started as an ache in my lower lip about 4 hours ago, and over the next couple of hours my lips and face blew up. It's still getting worse.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Swelling is deep, tense and burning rather than itchy, with no hives or welts on the skin
trigger_question_examples: Is the skin itchy or is there a rash or hives?
What does the swelling feel like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's not itchy at all — it feels tight and burning, like pressure. There's no rash or hives, just the swelling.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recently commenced a new antihypertensive ('-pril' / perindopril) approximately 10 days ago
trigger_question_examples: Have you started any new medications recently?
What blood pressure tablet did you start, and when?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — I started a blood pressure tablet about 10 days ago. I think it's perindopril, one of the '-pril' ones, 5 mg a day.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Swelling is asymmetric, involving the lips, right periorbital area and right cheek
trigger_question_examples: Is it on one side or both?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's mostly on the right — my lips, around my right eye and my right cheek.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Current airway status by self-report — speaking full sentences, no noisy breathing, no subjective breathlessness (internal airway not assessable remotely)
trigger_question_examples: Is your breathing noisy or hard?
Has your voice changed, or is it hard to swallow your saliva?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can talk in full sentences and my breathing feels okay — it's not noisy. My voice is just a bit muffled from my lips. I can still swallow.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No systemic features of an allergic reaction — no widespread hives, no wheeze, no faintness, no abdominal pain or vomiting
trigger_question_examples: Do you have hives all over, wheezing, tummy pain or feel faint?
Any dizziness or feeling you might pass out?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — no hives all over, no wheezing, my tummy is fine and I don't feel faint or dizzy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives about 30 minutes from the nearest hospital, with a partner present
trigger_question_examples: How far are you from a hospital?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My partner is here with me. We're about 30 minutes from the nearest hospital.
